title: Duty of Confidentiality
description: Maintain confidentiality of all information relating to client representation per ABA Model Rule 1.6.

title: Conflict of Interest Check
description: Identify and resolve conflicts of interest before accepting representation per Rule 1.7.

title: Former Client Conflicts
description: Avoid representing parties adverse to former clients in same or substantially related matters.

title: Imputed Disqualification
description: Address conflicts among lawyers in firm via Rule 1.10 imputation analysis.

title: Trust Account Segregation
description: Segregate client funds in IOLTA or non-interest-bearing trust account per Rule 1.15.

title: Trust Account Recordkeeping
description: Maintain trust account records for at least five years following representation.

title: Reasonable Fee Requirement
description: Charge only reasonable fees considering Rule 1.5 factors with written engagement letter.

title: Duty of Competence
description: Provide competent representation including legal knowledge, skill, thoroughness, and preparation.

title: Technology Competence
description: Maintain competence in technology relevant to legal practice per Rule 1.1 Comment 8.

title: Client Communication Duty
description: Keep client reasonably informed and respond to reasonable requests for information.

title: Reasonable Diligence
description: Act with reasonable diligence and promptness in representing clients per Rule 1.3.

title: Supervisory Lawyer Responsibility
description: Make reasonable efforts to ensure subordinate lawyer compliance with Rules.

title: Non-Lawyer Assistant Supervision
description: Ensure non-lawyer assistants comply with professional obligations per Rule 5.3.

title: Truthful Communication
description: Avoid false or misleading communication about lawyer services per Rule 7.1.

title: Candor to Tribunal
description: Exercise candor toward tribunal and not knowingly make false statements per Rule 3.3.

title: Fairness to Opposing Parties
description: Refrain from unlawfully obstructing access to evidence per Rule 3.4.

title: Unauthorized Practice Prohibition
description: Refrain from assisting unauthorized practice of law and limit jurisdiction-specific practice.

title: Fee Division With Other Lawyers
description: Limit fee division to proper circumstances with written client consent per Rule 1.5(e).

title: Mandatory Withdrawal
description: Withdraw from representation when continuing would violate rules per Rule 1.16(a).

title: Client File Retention
description: Retain client files for jurisdiction-required period and return promptly upon request.

title: Legal Service Lawful Basis
description: Document lawful basis for processing personal data in legal representation.

title: DSAR Response Within One Month
description: Respond to data subject access requests within one month with possible two-month extension.

title: Right to Erasure With Legal Hold
description: Honor right to erasure subject to legitimate retention for legal claims defense.

title: Granular Consent for Marketing
description: Obtain freely given specific consent for legal-services marketing communications.

title: 72-Hour Breach Notification
description: Notify supervisory authority of personal data breach within 72 hours.

title: SCC for Cross-Border Legal Work
description: Use Standard Contractual Clauses for cross-border data transfers in international cases.

title: DPIA for Large-Scale Processing
description: Conduct DPIA for systematic monitoring or large-scale processing of legal data.

title: DPO Where Required
description: Appoint DPO for large law firms with systematic monitoring or special category data.

title: Article 30 Records of Processing
description: Maintain records of processing activities for all controllers and processors.

title: Data Minimization Principle
description: Process only personal data adequate, relevant, and limited to representation purpose.

title: PECR Marketing Consent
description: Obtain consent before marketing electronic communications per ePrivacy Regulation.

title: Cookie Consent for Trackers
description: Obtain explicit consent for non-essential cookies and tracking technologies.

title: Law Firm ISMS
description: Establish ISMS scope covering client matter information and trust account systems.

title: Annual Risk Assessment
description: Perform documented information security risk assessment at least annually.

title: Client Data Encryption
description: Encrypt client data at rest and in transit per ISO 27001 Annex A.8.24.

title: Information Asset Inventory
description: Maintain inventory of client matter information assets with classification.

title: Vendor Security in Contracts
description: Address information security in vendor agreements with monitoring.

title: Information Security Incident Response
description: Maintain documented incident response with classification and escalation.

title: Secure Software Development
description: Apply secure development practices to legal software per Annex A.8.27.

title: Internal ISMS Audit
description: Conduct internal audits of ISMS at planned intervals.

title: Security Trust Services Criteria
description: Implement controls aligned with AICPA TSC Common Criteria for security.

title: Confidentiality Controls
description: Identify, classify, and protect confidential client information per AICPA TSC C1.

title: Role-Based Access Control
description: Implement RBAC with periodic review and least-privilege defaults.

title: Continuous Monitoring
description: Implement continuous monitoring of client data access with automated alerting.

title: Vendor Risk Management
description: Assess and monitor vendors with access to client systems.

title: Security Incident Response
description: Maintain incident response procedures with detection through post-incident review.

title: SOX-Compliant Documentation for Clients
description: Maintain SOX-compliant audit trail documentation when serving public-company clients.

title: FRCP Rule 26 Disclosures
description: Provide initial disclosures including ESI per Federal Rules of Civil Procedure Rule 26.

title: ESI Preservation Hold
description: Implement legal hold to preserve potentially relevant electronically stored information.

title: Proportionality in Discovery
description: Conduct discovery proportional to needs of case per FRCP 26(b)(1).

title: Privilege Log Maintenance
description: Maintain privilege log identifying withheld documents per FRCP 26(b)(5).

title: Spoliation Sanctions Avoidance
description: Avoid sanctions by preserving ESI when litigation reasonably anticipated.

title: FOIA Request Response
description: Respond to FOIA requests within 20 working days for federal agency clients.

title: Attorney-Client Privilege Tagging
description: Tag privileged communications in document management for protection during discovery.

title: Work-Product Doctrine Tagging
description: Tag attorney work product per Hickman v. Taylor doctrine.

title: GoBD-Compliant Record Retention
description: Retain digital records per German GoBD principles for German clients.

title: German Federal Data Protection
description: Apply BDSG specifications to GDPR processing for German clients and matters.

title: RVG Fee Calculation
description: Calculate fees per German Lawyers' Compensation Act (RVG) where applicable.

title: BRAO Professional Conduct
description: Comply with German Federal Lawyers' Act professional conduct rules.

title: Lawyer AML Reporting
description: Apply FATF Recommendations to lawyer AML for real estate, M&A, and trust services.

title: Trust Account BSA Compliance
description: Apply BSA AML controls to law firm trust accounts where required.

title: OFAC Client Screening
description: Screen prospective clients against OFAC SDN list before engagement.

title: FCPA Anti-Bribery Compliance
description: Avoid corrupt payments to foreign officials in international representations.

title: UK Bribery Act Compliance
description: Maintain adequate procedures to prevent bribery per Section 7 corporate offense.

title: AI Disclosure to Court
description: Disclose AI assistance in legal filings where required by court orders.

title: High-Risk Legal AI Classification
description: Classify legal AI tools per EU AI Act high-risk categories with conformity assessment.

title: AI Tool Competence
description: Maintain competence in AI tools used in legal practice including known limitations.

title: AI Output Supervision
description: Review AI-generated legal work product before client delivery or court filing.

title: AI Use Disclosure to Client
description: Disclose material AI use to clients per Rule 1.4 communication duties.

title: Multi-Jurisdictional Practice
description: Comply with bar admission requirements in each jurisdiction of practice.

title: Charging and Retaining Liens
description: Apply jurisdiction-appropriate charging and retaining lien rules with notice.

title: Client Property Safekeeping
description: Safeguard client property separately with appropriate insurance.

title: Fee Dispute Arbitration
description: Participate in mandatory fee dispute arbitration where required by jurisdiction.

title: Settlement Authority
description: Obtain client authority before settling with documented written confirmation.

title: Limited Scope Representation
description: Define limited scope clearly in writing and obtain client consent per Rule 1.2(c).

title: Inadvertent Disclosure
description: Take reasonable steps to prevent inadvertent disclosure of metadata in transmitted documents.

title: Metadata Mining Restrictions
description: Avoid mining metadata from inadvertently received documents per jurisdiction rules.

title: Social Media Conduct
description: Apply Rules to social media communications including duty of confidentiality.

title: Cloud Computing Due Diligence
description: Conduct due diligence on cloud providers handling confidential client information.

title: Reasonable Cybersecurity
description: Implement reasonable cybersecurity measures to protect client confidentiality.

title: Litigation Funder Disclosure
description: Disclose litigation funding arrangements per local court rules.

title: Third-Party Neutral Disclosure
description: Disclose role and inform unrepresented parties when serving as third-party neutral.

title: Truthful Negotiation Statements
description: Refrain from false statements of material fact in negotiations per Rule 4.1.

title: Communication With Represented Persons
description: Obtain consent from counsel before communicating with represented persons.

title: No Frivolous Claims
description: Refrain from advancing frivolous claims or defenses per Rule 3.1.

title: Jury Trial Restrictions
description: Refrain from improper communications with jurors and prospective jurors.

title: Trial Publicity Restrictions
description: Avoid extrajudicial statements likely to materially prejudice adjudicative proceedings.

title: Professional Misconduct Reporting
description: Report serious misconduct of other lawyers per Rule 8.3.

title: Judicial Misconduct Reporting
description: Report serious judicial misconduct per Rule 8.3(b).

title: Advance Fee Treatment
description: Treat advance fees as client funds in trust until earned per jurisdiction.

title: Non-Refundable Fee Limits
description: Apply Rule 1.5 reasonableness to non-refundable fee arrangements.

title: Contingency Fee Agreement
description: Document contingency fee in writing with method and deductible expenses.

title: Aggregate Settlement Disclosure
description: Disclose aggregate settlement details to each client per Rule 1.8(g).

title: Business Transactions With Clients
description: Document fair, reasonable business transactions with clients per Rule 1.8(a).

title: Substantial Gift Restrictions
description: Avoid soliciting substantial gifts from clients except family members per Rule 1.8(c).

title: Literary Rights Restrictions
description: Avoid acquiring literary or media rights to representation before conclusion.

title: Financial Assistance to Clients
description: Limit financial assistance to litigation expenses repayable from recovery.

title: Third-Party Payment Acceptance
description: Accept third-party payment only with informed client consent and no interference.

title: Client Relationship Restrictions
description: Avoid sexual relations with clients except pre-existing relationships.

title: California Consumer Privacy Rights
description: Honor CCPA rights for California consumer clients.
